contributors.expertise_basis: Full-text review of LH-Bench v2; this fixture demonstrates review-to-design transfer and does not represent a domain-expert interview.
Repository benchmark design artifacts and executable-contract implementation

claims.statement: Expert workflow expectations become more reliably judgeable when expressed as observable state transitions rather than adjectives.
claims.locator: LH-Bench review, Unique insight and Transferable design patterns 1

provenance.description: Full-text review extracts the procedure-to-evidence crosswalk and reports its limitations.

claims.statement: Private checks should test consequences of public professional principles rather than introduce surprise obligations.
claims.locator: LH-Bench review, Public/private split without hidden-rule unfairness

provenance.description: Review distinguishes held-out consequence checks from undisclosed formatting or content rules.

primitives.statement: Publicly require the recommendation to connect its decision to task evidence, execution evidence, artifact evidence, and diagnosis.
A polished recommendation that cites only aggregate scores can conceal the causal evidence needed to distinguish agent failure from grader or environment failure.

scenarios.decision: Whether to retain diagnostic traces or report only final artifact scores
scenarios.critical_incident: A failed agent run has an impressive final memo but incomplete evidence about whether the agent, environment, or grader caused the failure.

provenance.description: Repository review grounded in the locally preserved full v2 paper.

traps.mechanism: The source contains useful headline scores that can support a fluent recommendation without inspecting trace-to-diagnosis requirements.
traps.failure_signature: Recommendation proposes aggregate reporting but does not identify the trace, artifact, grader, and causal-diagnosis links needed for root-cause attribution.
traps.fairness_basis: The public artifact convention explicitly requires connecting decision, execution, artifact, and diagnostic evidence; the held-out check tests that consequence rather than an undisclosed obligation.

minimum_acceptable: Connects task evidence, trace evidence, artifact evidence, grader evidence, and root-cause diagnosis.
excellent: Distinguishes agent capability failures from environment, grader, and task-design failures.
Explains why aggregate scores alone are insufficient for operational diagnosis.

checks.description: The recommendation preserves enough linked evidence for causal diagnosis.
checks.observable_evidence: Named links among the task, execution trace, produced artifact, grader decision, and root-cause diagnosis in outputs/recommendation.md.
checks.pass_criteria: All five evidence-chain links are present and the recommendation explains their diagnostic role.
checks.fail_criteria: One or more links are absent, or only an aggregate score is offered.
failure_signatures: Agent failure asserted without excluding environment or grader failure

criteria: Claims are explicitly labeled as benchmark-review evidence, not domain-expert elicitation.
Every primitive maps to claims, a scenario, and a check.
Scenario specifies role, stakeholder, decision, and a capability-revealing critical incident.
Every source and trap has provenance and a fairness rationale.
Checks are executable and independently reviewed for observable boundaries and hidden-rule fairness.
quality_gates.notes: Contract skeleton only; no grader implementation or independent review yet.

criteria: At least one pilot run and expert adjudication test the intended failure signature.
Leakage, expert validity, reproducibility, and known limitations are reviewed.

known_limitations: This fixture demonstrates benchmark-review-to-design transfer; it does not substitute for domain-expert elicitation.
The rubric gate remains open because no executable grader or pilot has been run.
